title: Wee Willie Winkie
content:
Wee Willie Winkie.


Wee Willie Winkie runs through the town,
    Upstairs and downstairs in his nightgown,
Rapping at the window, crying through the lock,
"Are the children in their beds, for now it's eight o'clock?"
meaning:
This evocative piece by Leonard Leslie Brooke, titled "Wee Willie Winkie", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
